verses.text: Against Gog of Magog: God will turn him back, put hooks in his jaws, and bring him forth with all his army. Sovereign control. Even the great foe is led by a hook in God's hand.
Against Gog of Magog: God will turn him back, put hooks in his jaws, and bring him forth with all his army. Sovereign control. Even the great foe is led by a hook in God's hand.
Against Gog of Magog: God will turn him back, put hooks in his jaws, and bring him forth with all his army. Sovereign control. Even the great foe is led by a hook in God's hand.
Against Gog of Magog: God will turn him back, put hooks in his jaws, and bring him forth with all his army. Sovereign control. Even the great foe is led by a hook in God's hand.
Persia, Ethiopia, and Libya join him; let him prepare himself and his vast company. A mighty host gathers. The enemy seems overwhelming, yet he moves at God's summons.
Persia, Ethiopia, and Libya join him; let him prepare himself and his vast company. A mighty host gathers. The enemy seems overwhelming, yet he moves at God's summons.
Persia, Ethiopia, and Libya join him; let him prepare himself and his vast company. A mighty host gathers. The enemy seems overwhelming, yet he moves at God's summons.
After many days he comes against a land regathered and dwelling safely. Peace disturbed. God's people may yet face a final assault when most at rest.
After many days he comes against a land regathered and dwelling safely. Peace disturbed. God's people may yet face a final assault when most at rest.
He plots an evil thought, to fall upon 'the unwalled villages' and take a spoil. Greedy ambition. The foe is drawn by plunder against a defenceless people.
He plots an evil thought, to fall upon 'the unwalled villages' and take a spoil. Greedy ambition. The foe is drawn by plunder against a defenceless people.
He plots an evil thought, to fall upon 'the unwalled villages' and take a spoil. Greedy ambition. The foe is drawn by plunder against a defenceless people.
He plots an evil thought, to fall upon 'the unwalled villages' and take a spoil. Greedy ambition. The foe is drawn by plunder against a defenceless people.
When His people dwell safely, Gog comes from the north, 'that the heathen may know me.' God's purpose. Even the enemy's attack serves to reveal the LORD's glory.
When His people dwell safely, Gog comes from the north, 'that the heathen may know me.' God's purpose. Even the enemy's attack serves to reveal the LORD's glory.
When His people dwell safely, Gog comes from the north, 'that the heathen may know me.' God's purpose. Even the enemy's attack serves to reveal the LORD's glory.
Is this he of whom the prophets spoke? In that day God's fury rises, and there is 'a great shaking.' The Lord acts. The long-foretold conflict ends in God's intervention.
Is this he of whom the prophets spoke? In that day God's fury rises, and there is 'a great shaking.' The Lord acts. The long-foretold conflict ends in God's intervention.
Is this he of whom the prophets spoke? In that day God's fury rises, and there is 'a great shaking.' The Lord acts. The long-foretold conflict ends in God's intervention.
All creation trembles; sword turns against brother; God will 'magnify myself,' and they shall know He is the LORD. Self-destruction. The proud foe is undone, and God is glorified.
